title: Executive Order 9083—Redistribution of Maritime Functions

flags.justification: This order disperses functions across the Bureau of Customs and the Coast Guard rather than concentrating them, with each exercised 'under the direction and supervision of' the respective Secretary.
Section-by-section, highly specific functional assignments to named officials with clear supervisory chains (Treasury for Customs, Navy for Coast Guard).
Extensive technical detail, but all sections serve one coherent purpose: wartime reorganization of maritime regulatory functions.

The order transfers well-established, pre-existing regulatory functions (vessel inspection, licensing, registry) with their own already-defined legal standards; it reassigns administration rather than creating new standards.
The underlying vessel-safety and registry regime's existing incentive structure is undisturbed by this reassignment of administering officials.
Sec. 9 sets an explicit termination trigger: the order 'remain[s] in force until the termination of Title I of the First War Powers Act, 1941' — a well-designed, defined end-point.
The existing regulatory frameworks (inspection, licensing, casualty investigation) are preserved in substance; only the administering official changes.
Existing enforcement mechanisms (inspections, license suspension/revocation, casualty investigation boards) transfer intact with the functions themselves.
A routine wartime administrative reorganization of existing maritime functions; no new authority claim is made.

Despite redistributing one bureau's functions across two other agencies plus transfers from the Maritime Commission and Collectors of Customs, each section (2 through 5) specifies precisely what transfers where — careful, deliberate reallocation rather than the ambiguous overlap this flag targets.
Sec. 6 transfers an existing waiver authority (to waive navigation/inspection laws) to new officials; it does not create a new or broadened exemption regime.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/22 = 0.0%. A long, highly detailed order that scores cleanly precisely because of its care and specificity — length and technical density are not proxies for structural weight.
